questions.main_text:
The baby’s perambulator or pram was designed in 1733 by the Palladian architect William Kent for his client, the fourth Duke of Devonshire. To save any [SELECT id=241]  on the part of His Grace, the wheeled basket was designed to be pulled by a goat. [SELECT id=242]   in the mid-1950s, the first collapsible buggy was also English, Saffron Walden-born Owen Maclaren [SELECT id=243]   the undercarriage for the Supermarine Spitfire.

​

---

​

Bicycles are faster, lighter, use sexier materials and are [SELECT id=244]  hugely more expensive than ever before, but the arrangement of a diamond frame and two similar-sized wheels really hasn’t changed much since 1885. John Kemp Starley introduced the concept with his Rover safety cycle, a design which proved so influential that in Poland a bike is still referred [SELECT id=245]   as a ‘rower’.

content.text:
The baby’s perambulator or pram was designed in 1733 by the Palladian architect William Kent for his client, the fourth Duke of Devonshire. To save any [SELECT id=241]  on the part of His Grace, the wheeled basket was designed to be pulled by a goat. [SELECT id=242]   in the mid-1950s, the first collapsible buggy was also English, Saffron Walden-born Owen Maclaren [SELECT id=243]   the undercarriage for the Supermarine Spitfire.

​

---

​

Bicycles are faster, lighter, use sexier materials and are [SELECT id=244]  hugely more expensive than ever before, but the arrangement of a diamond frame and two similar-sized wheels really hasn’t changed much since 1885. John Kemp Starley introduced the concept with his Rover safety cycle, a design which proved so influential that in Poland a bike is still referred [SELECT id=245]   as a ‘rower’.

questions.main_text:
At the beginning of April 1789, HMS Bounty left Tahiti, [GAP id=246] plants to the West Indies. The ship had been in Tahiti since the previous autumn, and the crew had amused [GAP id=247] in the sunshine with the local women. But as they ploughed through [GAP id=248], they chafed at the restoration of discipline under the captain, William Bligh. By the night of 27 April, his old friend Fletcher Christian had decided to act.

​

In the early hours of the following morning, Christian and [GAP id=249] allies secured the upper deck and armed themselves with muskets. Some time after five o’clock, Christian led them to Bligh’s cabin. [GAP id=250] his own account, the captain “called [GAP id=251] I could in hopes of assistance”, but the mutineers managed to drag him away. [GAP id=252], the Bounty was in chaos. On the quarterdeck, [GAP id=253] mutineers, Bligh shouted for help, [GAP id=254] his shipmates to “knock Christian down”. Amid the general chaos, Christian exhorted the men to back him instead.

​

[GAP id=255] to Christian’s expectation, many of the men were determined to support the captain, not the mutineers, [GAP id=256] suggests Bligh’s reputation for hardness is ill deserved. In all, eighteen men [GAP id=257] in the ship’s launch. The carpenter’s mates wanted to go too, but Christian [GAP id=258] it. “Never fear lads,” Bligh said. “I’ll do you [GAP id=259], if ever I reach England.”

​

Bligh [GAP id=260]. reach England, months later. But the mutineers’ fate was wretched. Many were captured, others killed. Christian was killed on Pitcairn in 1793.

​

_Lähde: Dominic Sandbrook, BBC History Magazine, April 2016_

_Kuva: http://bit.ly/2jOdvVF (27.9.2016)_

content.text:
At the beginning of April 1789, HMS Bounty left Tahiti, [GAP id=246] plants to the West Indies. The ship had been in Tahiti since the previous autumn, and the crew had amused [GAP id=247] in the sunshine with the local women. But as they ploughed through [GAP id=248], they chafed at the restoration of discipline under the captain, William Bligh. By the night of 27 April, his old friend Fletcher Christian had decided to act.

​

In the early hours of the following morning, Christian and [GAP id=249] allies secured the upper deck and armed themselves with muskets. Some time after five o’clock, Christian led them to Bligh’s cabin. [GAP id=250] his own account, the captain “called [GAP id=251] I could in hopes of assistance”, but the mutineers managed to drag him away. [GAP id=252], the Bounty was in chaos. On the quarterdeck, [GAP id=253] mutineers, Bligh shouted for help, [GAP id=254] his shipmates to “knock Christian down”. Amid the general chaos, Christian exhorted the men to back him instead.

​

[GAP id=255] to Christian’s expectation, many of the men were determined to support the captain, not the mutineers, [GAP id=256] suggests Bligh’s reputation for hardness is ill deserved. In all, eighteen men [GAP id=257] in the ship’s launch. The carpenter’s mates wanted to go too, but Christian [GAP id=258] it. “Never fear lads,” Bligh said. “I’ll do you [GAP id=259], if ever I reach England.”

​

Bligh [GAP id=260]. reach England, months later. But the mutineers’ fate was wretched. Many were captured, others killed. Christian was killed on Pitcairn in 1793.

​

_Lähde: Dominic Sandbrook, BBC History Magazine, April 2016_

questions.main_text:
Koe koostuu neljästä osasta: kuullunymmärtämistehtävät, tekstin­ymmärtämistehtävät, sanasto- ja rakennetehtävät ja kirjallinen tuottaminen.

​

Voit tehdä tehtävät haluamassasi järjestyksessä. Noudata tehtäväkohtaisia vastaamisohjeita. Voit muuttaa antamiasi vastauksia koska tahansa kokeen aikana. Tee kukin tehtävä loppuun ennen kuin aloitat seuraavan.

​

Vastaa monivalintatehtäviin ja monivalinta-aukkotehtäviin napsauttamalla kulloinkin sopivinta vaihtoehtoa. Kirjoita avoimissa tehtävissä vastauksesi tehtävän yhteydessä olevaan vastauskenttään. Tehtävissä voi olla myös tehtäväkohtaista ohjeistusta.

​

Lisää kielikokeen vastausohjeita on linkissä [LINK url=http://yle.fi/plus/abitreenit/2018/kevat/EA-fi/EA-fi/attachments/Kielikokeen-ohjeistus.pdf|text=Kielikokeen ohjeistus].

​

Kuullunymmärtämistehtävien äänitteet ja videot saat käynnistää omaan tahtiisi napsauttamalla siihen liittyvää painiketta. Kun olet käyttänyt sallitut kuuntelukerrat, et voi enää kuunnella äänitettä uudelleen. Käytetyt painikkeet tunnistaa niiden muuttuneesta väristä. Varaa aikaa kuullun­ymmärtämistehtävien tekemiseen yhteensä noin 1 tunti.

​

Videot katsotaan aineistovälilehdellä olevalla mediasoittimella. Videoita voit katsoa niin monta kertaa kuin haluat. Voit myös pysäyttää videoiden toiston ja kelata niitä eteen- tai taaksepäin.

​

Joissakin tehtävissä on aineistoa, joka löytyy erilliseltä välilehdeltä. Sinne pääset [LINK url=http://yle.fi/plus/abitreenit/2018/kevat/EA-fi/EA-fi/attachments/index.html|text=tästä linkistä]. Yksittäisen tehtävän aineistoon pääset myös suoraan napsauttamalla kyseisen tehtävän yhteydessä olevaa Avaa aineisto -linkkiä.

​

Onnea kokeeseen!

​

Kun olet käyttänyt sallitut kuuntelukerrat, et voi enää kuunnella äänitettä uudelleen. Käytetyt painikkeet tunnistaa niiden muuttuneesta väristä. Varaa aikaa kuullun­ymmärtämistehtävien tekemiseen yhteensä noin 1 tunti.

​

Videot katsotaan aineistovälilehdellä olevalla mediasoittimella. Videoita voit katsoa niin monta kertaa kuin haluat. Voit myös pysäyttää videoiden toiston ja kelata niitä eteen- tai taaksepäin.

​

Joissakin tehtävissä on aineistoa, joka löytyy erilliseltä välilehdeltä. Sinne pääset [LINK url=http://yle.fi/plus/abitreenit/2018/kevat/EA-fi/EA-fi/attachments/index.html|text=tästä linkistä]. Yksittäisen tehtävän aineistoon pääset myös suoraan napsauttamalla kyseisen tehtävän yhteydessä olevaa Avaa aineisto -linkkiä.

​

Onnea kokeeseen!

questions.main_text:
**Choose a word that means about the same as the bold word and fits the context. (4 p.)**

​

Marenco said he left the military with emotional and physical problems, but he [SELECT id=237] **took advantage of** the G.I. Bill law to get his bachelor’s degree at Florida International University, with a double major in marketing and business administration. After months of not being able to find a job, he returned to the university to get his master’s degree in international business.

​

Again, he failed to get a job. So he turned his energy and focus to an idea that had been long [SELECT id=238] **brewing**, the [SELECT id=239] **creation** of personal development and self-help books. From all of his life experiences, he developed his idea for a life planner that also helps users organize and [SELECT id=240] **define** their values about the various aspects of their lives, including community and personal relationships, finances and even spirituality. Nowhere else had he seen a planner with this holistic approach.

content.text:
**Choose a word that means about the same as the bold word and fits the context. (4 p.)**

​

Marenco said he left the military with emotional and physical problems, but he [SELECT id=237] **took advantage of** the G.I. Bill law to get his bachelor’s degree at Florida International University, with a double major in marketing and business administration. After months of not being able to find a job, he returned to the university to get his master’s degree in international business.

​

Again, he failed to get a job. So he turned his energy and focus to an idea that had been long [SELECT id=238] **brewing**, the [SELECT id=239] **creation** of personal development and self-help books. From all of his life experiences, he developed his idea for a life planner that also helps users organize and [SELECT id=240] **define** their values about the various aspects of their lives, including community and personal relationships, finances and even spirituality. Nowhere else had he seen a planner with this holistic approach.

questions.main_text:
Choose a sentence for the gap that best fits the meaning of the whole paragraph. (2 p.)

​

_Some firms are trying to create some wellbeing, too, showering their employees with mindfulness courses, yoga lessons and anything else that proves that managers are interested in “the whole person”. [SELECT id=218] Management theorists note that a big threat to corporate performance is widespread disengagement among workers. Happy people are more engaged and productive, say psychologists. Gallup claimed in 2013 that the “unhappiness” of employees costs the American economy $500 billion a year in lost productivity._

content.text:
Choose a sentence for the gap that best fits the meaning of the whole paragraph. (2 p.)

​

_Some firms are trying to create some wellbeing, too, showering their employees with mindfulness courses, yoga lessons and anything else that proves that managers are interested in “the whole person”. [SELECT id=218] Management theorists note that a big threat to corporate performance is widespread disengagement among workers. Happy people are more engaged and productive, say psychologists. Gallup claimed in 2013 that the “unhappiness” of employees costs the American economy $500 billion a year in lost productivity._

choices.text: However, other companies would be willing to go even further.
However, only the misguided would take that at face value.
However, such action has been criticized for being too expensive.

It is being used by the aboriginal youth everywhere in the country
It is in competition with other musical genres in some communities
